opportunities.title: Humanity Protocol Discovers Operational Security After Losing $36M — A Field Guide to Post-Hack PR
opportunities.why_now: The protocol is announcing security 'prioritization' literally days after a $36M breach, the textbook reactive-security pattern this channel exists to dissect.
opportunities.angle: Post-mortem breakdown: what 'prioritizing opsec' actually means after the money's gone, with a checklist of what should've been in place pre-hack

opportunities.title: No, AI Didn't Cause a DeFi Hackpocalypse — The Data Says Something Worse
opportunities.why_now: A Dragonfly partner is publicly pushing back on AI-hack panic narratives right as the market wants a scapegoat, giving this channel a contrarian, data-grounded angle.
opportunities.angle: Skeptical explainer: pull the actual exploit data, show hacks are still old-fashioned key/logic failures, and argue AI fear is distracting from real opsec basics

opportunities.title: Meet 'Stern': The Ransomware Kingpin Who Ran Crime Like a Fortune 500 Company
opportunities.why_now: EU sanctions just landed on the alleged operator behind Trickbot/Conti-linked billions in damage, a rare named-and-shamed moment worth unpacking for a security-literate audience.
opportunities.angle: Profile/explainer on the sanctioned operator's business model and what the sanction actually changes (or doesn't) for on-chain enforcement

opportunities.title: Treasury Just Froze $130M in Iran-Linked Wallets — Here's Why That's Scarier Than a Hack
opportunities.why_now: A fresh $130M freeze shows the state's tracing/seizure capability keeps outpacing 'privacy coin' assumptions, a direct hit to the 'crypto is untraceable' myth this channel loves to puncture.
opportunities.angle: Explainer on how the freeze was technically possible, tying it to the broader thesis that on-chain funds are more seizable than believed

opportunities.title: The US Government Just Moved $288M in Seized Crypto to Coinbase Prime — Who's Actually Guarding It Now?
opportunities.why_now: A quarter-billion in seized assets landing in an exchange custody arm is a fresh, concrete hook for questioning custodial security assumptions around government-held crypto.
opportunities.angle: Security-angle explainer on custody chain-of-custody risk when state seizures rely on private exchange infrastructure
